concerns.text: Tasks T012, T013, and T014 implement a 150/150 sample collection strategy, directly violating the hard constraints of FR-001 (≥1000 human) and FR-002 (≥50 LLM) and SC-001 (1050 total) defined in spec.md. While the plan.md documents a methodological deviation, the tasks themselves silently relax the spec's explicit numerical requirements without a corresponding 'staged' or 'deviation' tag in the task description, constituting a constraint violation.

Task T014 sets a validation target of 143 samples (95% of 150) for each group. This contradicts SC-001 which requires a minimum of 947 human and 47 LLM samples. The task's verification step will pass a dataset that fails the specification's absolute sample count requirements, effectively weakening the acceptance criterion.

Task T026 implements a 'Blocked Permutation Test' but the spec's US-3 Acceptance Scenario 1 explicitly mandates a 'Shapiro-Wilk test' followed by 'Mann-Whitney U' or 'Welch's t-test'. The task replaces the specified statistical method with a different one (justified in plan.md) but fails to preserve the spec's acceptance criterion in the task's verification step, creating a semantic weakening of the requirement.

Task T012 implements logic to extract '3 fresh functions per repo' to reach 150 samples. FR-001 explicitly requires extracting '≥20 such functions per repository' to reach the 1000 sample target. The task scope is a silent narrowing of the data collection requirement, violating the spec's defined scope.
